message:
fix: avoid Dolt joinIter hangs in GetReadyWork blocker computation

Three changes to improve bd ready performance on databases with many wisps:

1. Replace correlated NOT EXISTS subquery with JOIN (deferred parent
   exclusion, GH#1190) with pre-computed ID set using separate
   single-table queries — consistent with the pattern used by
   computeBlockedIDs to avoid Dolt joinIter panics.

2. Add includeWisps parameter to computeBlockedIDs. When false (default
   for bd ready without --include-ephemeral), skip scanning wisps and
   wisp_dependencies tables entirely. Verified zero cross-table blocking
   deps exist (issues only blocked by issues, wisps only by wisps).
   Reduces data scanned from ~50K rows to ~500 rows.

3. Cache-aware: a full (wisps-included) cached result satisfies
   issues-only requests; invalidation clears the new flag.

Root cause of bd ready timeouts: the hq database has 18K+ wisps and
33K+ wisp_dependencies. computeBlockedIDs was scanning all of them
even when bd ready only displays non-ephemeral issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -88,6 +88,7 @@ type DoltStore struct {
 	blockedIDsCache    []string        // cached result of computeBlockedIDs
 	blockedIDsCacheMap map[string]bool
 	blockedIDsCached   bool // true once blockedIDsCache has been populated
+	blockedIDsCacheIncludesWisps bool // true if cache was computed with wisps
 	cacheMu            sync.Mutex
 
 	// OTel span attribute cache (avoids per-call allocation)
